Okay, let's see. The classic river crossing puzzle! The farmer has to get across the river with his fox, chicken, and bag of grain. The boat can only carry him and one item at a time. If he leaves the fox and chicken alone, the fox will eat the chicken. If he leaves the chicken and grain alone, the chicken will eat the grain. If he leaves the fox and grain alone, the fox will eat the grain. So, how can he get everything across safely?

Okay, let me think. So, the problem is that the farmer needs to get himself, the fox, the chicken, and the grain across the river safely. The boat can only carry him and one item at a time. So, he can't take two items at the same time. So, he needs to figure out a way to get all three across safely.

Let me think. So, the key here is that the fox will eat the chicken if left alone, and the chicken will eat the grain if left alone. So, the farmer can't leave the fox and chicken together, or the chicken and grain together. So, he needs to figure out a way to get all three across safely.
